titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Lee Wulff Papers
titlestmt.author: by Karen Spicher, Brooke McManus

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0., has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 7186931.
This collection contains 5 published digital objects. And those objects are comprised of 595 digital files.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday October 2, 2025 at 23:06

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The Lee Wulff Papers contain drafts, setting copies, and proofs of books, and drafts of magazine articles and reports by Wulff, with related correspondence and reviews; correspondence and related printed material; biographical clippings and obituaries; photographs of Wulff and his family, and photographs of early paintings by him; and flies and fly lines made by Wulff or designed by him. Research strengths include Wulff's writings on fishing, hunting, and conservation; catch and release fishing; fly fishing techniques, equipment, education, and the Wulff School of Fly Fishing; Wulff's design and marketing of fly fishing equipment, and Royal Wulff Products; hunting and fishing in Newfoundland and Labrador; conservation of the Atlantic salmon; and hunting, fishing, and conservation organizations in the United States and Canada, 1930s-1990s.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into nine series: I. Writings, 1938-1999. II. Correspondence and Subject Files, 1930s-2005. III. Photographs, 1914-1988. IV. Objects, undated. V. October 2014 acquisition, 1935-1998. VI. October 2016 acquisition, undated. VII. January 2018 acquisition, 1906-2009. VIII. January 2020 acquisition, 1937-2019. IX. March 2021 acquisition, 2019-2020.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.
Box 21 (objects): Restricted fragile material. Reference images may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Boxes 14, 23, 52 (DVDs): Restricted fragile. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 23 (DVDs): Access is restricted to within the Beinecke Library.
Boxes 49-51 (audiovisual material): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Lee Wulff Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

Box 23: Use of these films is limited to viewing by individuals or groups within the Beinecke Library. No part of these two films may be copied, sold, rented, shared online, or otherwise disseminated without the permission of The Wulff Projects, LLC.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Joan Wulff on the Jonathan O. Bulkley Fund, 2005 and 2018; gift of Joan Wulff, 2008-2020; gift of Barry Wulff, 2016; and gift of Stephen Jones on behalf of Joan Wulff, 2021.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Lee Wulff Papers. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: This collection consists of materials formerly classed as Uncat MSS 802, Uncat MSS 1060, and Uncat MSS 1241. In 2013, the collection received a baseline level of processing, including partial rehousing and minimal organization. The collection was reclassed under one call number, materials were organized into series, and containers were renumbered in a single sequence. Most folder titles were based on information provided by the donor, while some were assigned by Library staff. Folder titles were not verified against contents.

bioghist.head: Lee Wulff (1905-1991)
bioghist.p: Fly fisher, author, conservationist, artist, and filmmaker. Wulff invented fly fishing techniques and equipment, advocated catch and release fishing and Atlantic salmon conservation, and popularized fishing and hunting through his writings and films. He married Joan Salvato Wulff (1926-), fly fisher and author, with whom he founded the Wulff School of Fly Fishing, on the Beaverkill River, New York, in 1979, and Royal Wulff Products, a fly fishing equipment business.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Lee Wulff Papers contain drafts, setting copies, and proofs of books, and drafts of magazine articles and reports by Wulff, with related correspondence and reviews; correspondence and related printed material; biographical clippings and obituaries; photographs of Wulff and his family, and photographs of early paintings by him; and flies and fly lines made by Wulff or designed by him. Research strengths include Wulff's writings on fishing, hunting, and conservation; catch and release fishing; fly fishing techniques, equipment, education, and the Wulff School of Fly Fishing; Wulff's design and marketing of fly fishing equipment, and Royal Wulff Products; hunting and fishing in Newfoundland and Labrador; conservation of the Atlantic salmon; and hunting, fishing, and conservation organizations in the United States and Canada, 1930s-1990s.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three subseries: Books, Magazine Articles, and Other Writings. Each subseries is arranged alphabetically by title, followed by unsorted materials.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Writings concern fly fishing, hunting, and conservation, some with related correspondence, photographs, or research materials.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Memoir
Working title: Yellowbird

did.unittitle: Draft, computer printout with manuscript revisions, "corrected MS"
Drafts, mostly typescript carbon and computer printout; notes; and research materials
Drafts, mostly typescript carbon and computer printout; notes; and research materials
Draft, typescript carbon and typescript photocopy, with manuscript revisions, "Old MS"
Draft, typescript photocopy, with manuscript notes by Allan Wulff
Draft, typescript photocopy, with manuscript notes by John McDonald
Draft, typescript photocopy, with manuscript revisions and notes by Nick Lyon
Draft, typescript photocopy, with manuscript revisions and notes by Nick Lyon, "Yellowbird and Salmon"
Draft, typescript with manuscript revisions, photocopy

title.part: The Complete Lee Wulff: A Treasury of Lee Wulff's Greatest Angling Adventures

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Essay collection
Working title: The Essential Lee Wulff

did.unittitle: Draft, computer printout, with manuscript revisions, "revised copy"
Draft, typescript carbon, and page proof

title.part: Lee Wulff's New Handbook of Freshwater Fishing
Let's Go Fishing: A Boy's Book on Freshwater Fishing

did.unittitle: Draft, computer printout with manuscript revisions, and correspondence

title.part: Salmon on a Fly: The Essential Wisdom and Lore from a Lifetime of Salmon Fishing

did.unittitle: Draft, "I," computer printout with manuscript revisions

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Most magazine articles are typescript or typescript photocopy, with manuscript revisions

did.unittitle: Other magazine articles. Notes and drafts
Other magazine articles, "Not on any list." Clippings.
"Bourke Journal." Typescript photocopy, with photocopies of related documents and photographs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Transcript of a journal by Joseph A. Bourke about a trip from Brooklyn to Alaska during the 1898 gold rush, with Bill Wagner and Charlie Wulff, Lee Wulff's father

did.unittitle: "Man and the Animals." Drafts, typescript and typescript carbon; notes; correspondence; and research materials
Reports for Canadian government: "Newfoundland Government Business"
"Rivers in My Mind." Notes and drafts

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Collection of essays about rivers

relatedmaterial.head: Related Archival Materials note
relatedmaterial.p: Additional correspondence is in Series I, Writings.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Files contain mostly correspondence; also present are notes, clippings, and other printed material. Correspondents include publishers, fly fishing equipment businesses, fishing and conservation organizations, friends, colleagues, and fans. Some files contain biographical information, family correspondence, or other family papers.

did.unittitle: Lee Wulff Master Collection (Royal Wulff Products)

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: DVDs: use of originals is restricted. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 14 films, originally 16mm, on 2 DVDs

did.unittitle: "Fly Lines, Reels, Rods, Lee's Turtle Trap (as a child)"
Certificate of Membership in the New England Regional Fishery Management Council

relatedmaterial.head: Related Archival Materials note
relatedmaterial.p: Additional photographs are in Series I, Writings.

did.unittitle: Photographs of Lee Wulff, Joan Wulff, and other family

descriptivenote.p: Photographs of Lee Wulff, Joan Wulff, and other family

did.unittitle: Photographs of paintings by Lee Wulff

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: With container and instructions

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Container and instructions only

did.physdesc: 22 flies, some with identification tags or notes

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference images may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This acquisition consists of writings and illustrations for works such as Lee Wulff on Flies and Lee Wulff's Handbook of Freshwater Fishing ; correspondence and subject files on fishing, flying, and other activities; artwork including early drawings and paintings, commercial art, and film titles; photographs; objects such as a compass, woodblocks, and Ray-Ban sunglasses; and audiovisual material.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into six subseries: Writings, Correspondence and Subject Files, Artwork, Photographs, Objects, and Audiovisual and Computer Media.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Joan Wulff on the Jonathan O. Bulkley Fund, 2018.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries consists of illustrations for books by Wulff, an illustrated proposal to write a column for The News , notebooks and diaries, and other writings.

did.unittitle: Illustrated author/artist's proposal for The News

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Some include drawings by Wulff.

did.unittitle: Lee Wulff on Flies , black and white illustrations, ink on card
Lee Wulff's Handbook of Freshwater Fishing , ink & wash illustrations
"Shellbird Island Shoal," article in Field & Stream
Trout on a Fly , setting copy, typescript and photocopy, corrected

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries consists of correspondence, publicity files, materials relating to flying, films, and flies patented by Wulff, maps of Newfoundland, reference works on cinematography, and files on speaking engagements and events honoring Wulff.

did.unittitle: Great Lakes Carbon Corp. to whom Lee sold his camps

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p:
Includes some early poetry submissions and rejections from College Humor and Vanity
Fair .

did.unittitle: Early 1920s personal correspondence (friends) various people (some business letters)
Early 1920s personal correspondence (friends) various people (some business letters)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Primarily correspondence with Thomas D. White, as well as photographs and documents related to the United States Air Force and Sandhill River Salmon Club.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Assorted materials related to personal and professional matters, including patents and films by Wulff, and a portrait of Wulff at a one and a half years of age.

did.unittitle: AOPA Air-Aid Cruising Altitudes (MLS) For Magnetic Courses Ruler

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Associated material: A leather case formerly housing this material can be found in Box 41.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Associated material: A leather case formerly housing this material can be found in Box 41.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Associated material: A leather case formerly housing this material can be found in Box 41.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Associated material: A leather case formerly housing this material can be found in Box 41.

did.unittitle: Canada Department of Transport Commercial Pilot License leather wallet

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Enclosed are a license renewal certificate from Canada, 1959, and a United States Coast Guard identification card, 1942.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Associated material: A leather case formerly housing this material can be found in Box 41.

did.unittitle: Books - pilot biographies and history of Wings Club
Identification cards, licenses, and business cards
Ledgers, memo books, and address books

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Early files sometimes include writings, correspondence, ephemera, and passports.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a class notebook and diploma from Stanford.

did.unittitle: Biography of Lee by Jack Samson
Reference materials on cinematography and design
Reference materials on cinematography and design
Lee Wulff Testimonial Dinner, Anglers' Club
Jimmy Carter visit to Catskills [correspondence, photos, clippings]
LW 80th birthday scrapbook compiled by Neil Marvin

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Materials were removed from a binder and rehoused in folders by library staff at the time of processing.

did.unittitle: LW 80th birthday card from Dolly and Francis Davis, cover illustration by Francis Davis
LW 85th birthday celebration at Waldorf-Astoria Hotel

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, invitations, clippings, programs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries consists of pencil drawings by Wulff while he was studying art in Paris, advertising art, book covers, and film titles he created, a self-portrait, and a small amount of artwork by others.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain order found at time of acquisition.

did.unittitle: Early illustrations: Pastel drawings exhibited at Sunwise Turn book shop in 1928, Paris art, commercial art
Early illustrations: Pastel drawings exhibited at Sunwise Turn book shop in 1928, Paris art, commercial art

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains personal and professional photographs of Lee Wulff and Joan Salvato Wulff. Some photographs taken during Wulff's earlier years were printed at a later date. Dates in this subseries represent the year the photographs were taken, not when they were printed.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes several bills for work done and maintenance on his airplane, 1954-1955.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes images of George C. Marshall, Thomas White, and Jerry Brant, either fishing or in an outdoor environment.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Documents including a copy of World War I bond certificate, 1943 notice of classification to Wulff, permits from the United States Selective Service System to travel to Canada, and licenses and permits pertaining to photography and fishing from the Canadian government.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes artwork by the Wulffs.

did.unittitle: Photos from The Atlantic Salmon , first salmon book

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Not arranged in any particular order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries includes a set of flies designed by Wulff, woodblock stamps, a compass, and accessories such as a baseball cap and Ray-Ban sunglasses.

did.unittitle: "Flies by Lee Wulff's New and Better Method"
Navy baseball cap embroidered "NY QB Lee Wulff" with wings pin attached

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Associated material: Papers formerly housed in this case can be found in Box 33.

did.unittitle: Pin engraved "North Atlantic Division Fort Pepperrell," with inlaid photograph captioned "Lee Wulff Supt of Photography 4182"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains audio and video cassettes, long-playing records, and one DVD. The bulk of materials are educational films on fishing by the production company of Lee Wulff and Joan Salvato Wulff.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged by format and alphabetically by title therein.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.unittitle: Demo tape - 60 minute fly fishing series

Sound FX - Casting film / B. J. Murray
"Stories of the Atlantic Salmon," read by Lee Wulff [2 copies]

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.unittitle: "A World Record Marlin & San Juan River Tarpon"
Fishing Tales with Mike Sakamoto: KGMB TV-9 / Fishing for Steelhead on a dryfly: British Col. Canada
"I was Born to Fish: Lee Wulff on the Beaverkill"
John Groth & Lee on Sutton River in Ontario for ABC, 1973
"Labrador: Trophy Brook Trout and Atlantic Salmon"
Lee Wulff dinner Feb. 10 1990 Waldorf Astoria
Sunday Morning News Wonderful Lives / Chas. Kuralt
"Three Trout to Dream About and Minipi's Discovery"

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Access copies of computer files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.unittitle: Photographs of Lee Wulff at Hancock Airport
"Trout On A Fly" uncorrected page proof with holograph annotations
Canada's House of Commons' Atlantic Salmon Hall of Fame award, given posthumously, with congratulations correspondence
"Tuna Tournament on the High Seas" New York Times coverage featuring Lee Wulff with holograph note
Advertisements and brochures featuring Lee Wulff, including for "Motion Picture Lectures on the Outdoor Sports"
Typescript and holograph personal rod history and partial holograph correspondence
"Lee Wulff: A Remarkable Life" film advertisement and list of American Sportsman films
"The Best Way to Hunt Whitetail" article by Lee Wulff featured in "The American Sportsman"
"...as American as bait casting..." article by Lee Wulff featured in "The American Sportsman"

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Use of these films is limited to viewing by individuals or groups within the Beinecke Library. No part of these two films may be copied, sold, rented, shared online, or otherwise disseminated without the permission of The Wulff Projects, LLC.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Access is restricted to within the Beinecke Library.
Restricted fragile. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
